message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The dramatically expanding COVID-19 needs multiple effective countermeasures. Neutralizing antibodies are a potential therapeutic strategy for treating COVID-19. A number of neutralizing nanobodies (Nbs) were reported for their
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  activities. However,
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  protection of these nanobodies was not reported in animal models. In the current report, we characterized several RBD-specific Nbs isolated from a screen of an Nb library derived from an alpaca immunized with SARS-CoV-2 spike glycoprotein (S); among them, three Nbs exhibited picomolar potency against SARS-CoV-2 live virus, pseudotyped viruses, and 15 circulating SARS-CoV-2 variants. To improve the efficacy, various configurations of Nbs were engineered. Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  , a novel trimer constituted of three Nbs, was constructed to be bispecific for human serum albumin (HSA) and RBD of SARS-CoV-2. Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  exhibited sub-ng/ml neutralization potency against the wild-type and currently circulating variants of SARS-CoV-2 with a long half-life
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  . In addition, we showed that intranasal administration of Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  provided 100% protection for both prophylactic and therapeutic purposes against SARS-CoV-2 infection in transgenic hACE2 mice. Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                  -Nb
                  <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                  is a potential candidate for both prevention and treatment of SARS-CoV-2 through respiratory administration.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>One sentence summary</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Nb
                    <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                    -Nb
                    <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                    -Nb
                    <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                    , with a novel heterotrimeric bispecific configuration, exhibited potent and broad neutralization potency against SARS-CoV-2
                    <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                    and provided
                    <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                    protection against SARS-CoV-2 infection in hACE2 transgenic mice via intranasal delivery.
                  </jats:p>
                  <jats:fig id="ufig1" position="float" fig-type="figure" orientation="portrait">
                    <jats:caption>
                      <jats:title>Graphical abstract:</jats:title>
                    </jats:caption>
                    <jats:graphic xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="429275v1_ufig1" position="float" orientation="portrait"/>
                  </jats:fig>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        We described a novel heterotrimeric configuration of Nb-Nb
                        <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                        -Nb (Nb
                        <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                        -Nb
                        <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                        -Nb
                        <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                        ) that exhibited improved viral inhibition and stability.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Nb
                        <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                        -Nb
                        <jats:sub>H</jats:sub>
                        -Nb
                        <jats:sub>15</jats:sub>
                        provides ultrahigh neutralization potency against SARS-CoV-2 wild type and 18 mutant variants, including the current circulating variants of D614G and N501Y predominantly in the UK and South Africa.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>It is the first to demonstrate the Nbs efficacy in preventing and treating SARS-CoV-2 infection in hACE2 transgenic mice via intranasal delivery.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: A potent bispecific nanobody protects hACE2 mice against SARS-CoV-2 infection via intranasal administration
